rationale: The citing paper uses Cavanaugh (2014) in its core theoretical development, not just in a background list. Specifically, it cites Cavanaugh as evidence that feelings of deservingness can function as purchase justification, which directly supports the proposed entitlement-to-justification mechanism behind the price entitlement effect. It also cites Cavanaugh when arguing that justification pressures are weaker when social costs are absent, linking to a key boundary-condition argument. Because these citations help frame hypotheses and mechanism logic, the role is substantive.
relation_description: Lee and Winterich rely on Cavanaugh (2014) as prior evidence that deservingness-based feelings can justify indulgent consumption choices. They build from that logic to theorize that high prices induce entitlement/deservingness among upper-class consumers, which then justifies purchasing socially costly products.
